headline: WHO pandemic virus-sharing talks deadlock in Geneva.
summary: After another week of negotiations, WHO member states failed to agree on the Pathogen Access and Benefit Sharing (PABS) annex to the pandemic accord; talks extended by one year.

headline: Bilaterals III enters parliamentary phase.
summary: The Federal Council submitted the dispatch to parliament on 13 March; the spring session is now working through four federal resolutions: stabilisation of existing CH–EU agreements plus new accords on electricity, health, and food safety.

headline: Lausanne and Geneva housing crisis intensifies.
summary: A wave of lease terminations for renovation is hitting both cities simultaneously; vacancy rates are below 1% in both Vaud and Geneva, making relocation near-impossible.

headline: HEP Vaud governance crisis.
summary: Staff at the Haute École Pédagogique Vaud — French-speaking Switzerland's largest teacher-training institution — publicly denounce a "climate of fear," opaque decision-making, and retaliation risk for raising concerns internally.

headline: CH–US tariff talks: negotiations continuing.
summary: Federal Council confirmed in March that talks proceed under the January 2026 mandate; Switzerland committed to facilitate at least USD 200 bn in US-bound investment over five years; no breakthrough announced.

headline: Iran conflict day 65 — "Project Freedom" tests the ceasefire.
summary: Trump on Sunday announced a US naval mission to escort ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: Iran's 14-point peace proposal rejected.
summary: Tehran's plan calls for US security guarantees, troop withdrawal from the region, sanctions relief, war reparations, and a new Hormuz management mechanism — all within 30 days.

headline: Taiwan: largest-ever US arms package approved.
summary: Washington confirmed a USD 11.1 bn weapons sale — the biggest to date — drawing sharp objections from Beijing.

headline: Ireland joins Artemis Accords.
summary: A ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington at 15:00 EDT today formalises Ireland's accession.

headline: Cambrian fossil trove in southern China — half the species new to science.
summary: Remarkably preserved late-Cambrian specimens (~490 Ma) offer an unusually complete window into early animal diversity; paleontologists report approximately half the identified species are previously unknown.

headline: Engineered red-blood-cell clots stop bleeding in seconds.
summary: Researchers crosslinked modified red blood cells into tough, adhesive haemostatic clots that form within seconds and subsequently promote tissue regeneration — a potential advance for trauma surgery and wound care.

headline: Nine-protein plasma panel predicts APOL1 kidney risk.
summary: A biomarker signature outperforms existing clinical and genetic tools for predicting kidney disease events and mortality in individuals with high-risk APOL1 genotypes — a variant disproportionately affecting people of African descent.

— *Trees to Flows and Back: Unifying Decision Trees and Diffusion Models* (Ramachandran & Sra) — Proves a mathematical correspondence between hierarchical decision trees and diffusion processes via Global Trajectory Score Matching (GTSM); practical instantiation TreeFlow achieves competitive tabular

— *Information-geometric adaptive sampling for graph diffusion* — ICML 2026 accepted; applies information-geometric principles to adaptively sample within graph diffusion processes; extends naturally to graph generation and representation learning.

— *A unified perspective on fine-tuning and sampling with diffusion and flow models* (Domingo-Enrich, Du, Albergo) — Brings together previously separate frameworks for post-training alignment and inference-time steering; relevant to efficient and steerable generative inference.

— *Gradient Regularized Newton Boosting Trees with Global Convergence* (Zozoulenko et al.) — Adds gradient regularisation to Newton boosting to obtain provably global convergence guarantees; closes a longstanding theoretical gap for tree-based ensembles.

headline: Asian session: Hang Seng lower; Nikkei closed.
summary: Japanese markets are shut for Greenery Day (Golden Week runs through 5 May); last Nikkei close (Fri 1 May) was ~59,513 +0.4%.

headline: CHF slightly weaker on EUR.
summary: EUR/CHF trading near 0.924 (vs ~0.917 at Friday's close); USD/CHF near 0.781.

headline: US futures modestly green.
summary: S&P 500 futures +0.1%, Nasdaq futures +0.4% pre-open; Dow futures marginally negative.
